description_long: This is a weathered, ancient-style helmet reminiscent of those worn by Roman or Greek soldiers, featuring a prominent crest running from front to back. The helmet is primarily metallic, with a dark, tarnished steel appearance and visible streaks of rust and blood stains, suggesting it has seen battle. The front has a T-shaped opening for the eyes and mouth, providing both protection and visibility. The sides are adorned with subtle embossed circular motifs, and the crest is solid, arching upward and backward, ending in a rounded knob. The edges of the helmet are slightly gilded, adding a faint golden trim to the otherwise dark and battered surface. The overall form is robust and fully encloses the head, with no visible ventilation or modern attachments.
description: A battered, ancient-style metal helmet with a prominent crest, dark steel finish, and blood stains, evoking a battle-worn appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full arch of the crest, blood stains, and circular motif on the side.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the T-shaped eye and mouth opening and the symmetrical crest.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar features to view 0, showing the crest's curve and weathered surface.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the crest and the helmet's rounded, solid back.
